arts.ttl: Why Bukayo Saka was left out of Arsenal team to face Bournemouth
arts.sum: Bukayo Saka was omitted from the Arsenal matchday squad to face Bournemouth in the Premier League on Saturday. The winger was a slight doubt heading into the game after picking up an injury on international duty. He was taken off in the 51st minute of England’s 2-1 defeat to Greece at Wembley last Thursday. arts.ttl: Why is Bukayo Saka not playing for Arsenal today against Bournemouth? Injury update
arts.sum: Bukayo Saka is not playing for Arsenal against Bournemouth due to the fact that he has not recovered from the injury that has kept him out of action since the international break. He also added that Ødegaard is not going to be fit for the tie against Bournemouth, saying “I think he’s not going to be fit for this game.

arts.ttl: 'Earn the right to play' - Mikel Arteta fires ruthless message to struggling Arsenal star - football.london
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta has told Gabriel Jesus, he must "earn the right to play more" as he aims to get regular minutes following his injury setback last August. He's got our full support, he's a player that can play in various positions and he's a really important player for us."
